explanation: The statement "The autopilot shall prop_0 when a failure occurs (prop_1)" means that at any point in time, if a failure (prop_1) is present, then the autopilot must be performing prop_0 at that same point in time. This is a global implication.

The statement "The autopilot shall prop_0 when prop_1" means that if `prop_1` is true at any point, then `prop_0` must also be true at that same point. This is a global implication, translated as `G (prop_1 -> prop_0)`.

The statement "The autopilot shall prop_0 when a failure occurs (prop_1)" implies that at any point in time, if a failure (prop_1) occurs, then the autopilot must immediately prop_0 (prop_0). This represents a direct and concurrent implication. Therefore, we use the global operator (G) to ensure this implication holds at all times: if prop_1 is true, then prop_0 must also be true at that same moment.

The phrase "A when B" typically translates to "whenever B is true, A must also be true at that same moment." In LTL, this is expressed as a global implication: G(B -> A). Therefore, "The autopilot shall prop_0 when prop_1" means that globally, if prop_1 is true, then prop_0 must also be true.
The statement 'prop_0 when prop_1' implies that whenever prop_1 is true, prop_0 must also be true at that instant. This is a direct implication.

The statement "A shall B when C" means that if C is true, then B must also be true at the same time. This is a direct implication. Therefore, "The autopilot shall prop_0 when prop_1" translates to "if prop_1 is true, then prop_0 must be true at that instant".

The statement "The autopilot shall prop_0 when a failure occurs (prop_1)" means that at any point in time, if a failure (prop_1) occurs, then the autopilot (prop_0) must also be true at that exact same time. This is best represented by a global implication.

The statement "The autopilot shall prop_0 when prop_1 (apfail)" indicates a global implication. It means that whenever prop_1 (apfail) is true, prop_0 must also be true. This translates directly to a global implication: G (prop_1 -> prop_0).

The phrase "A when B" typically translates to an implication "B -> A", meaning that if B is true, then A must also be true at that same point in time. Therefore, "The autopilot shall prop_0 when prop_1" becomes "prop_1 implies prop_0".

The statement "The autopilot shall prop_0 when a failure occurs (prop_1)" means that if a failure (prop_1) is occurring, then the autopilot must be in the state prop_0 at that exact same time. This is a condition that must hold true at all times, hence the use of the global operator 'G'. The 'when' indicates a direct implication. Therefore, for every moment in time, if 'prop_1' is true, then 'prop_0' must also be true.
